items.title: When the Trump administration cracks down on Anthropic, who benefits?
items.summary: On the new episode of Equity, we discussed what actually prompted the administration's latest moves against Anthropic, and what this might mean for the AI ecosystem.

items.title: Beyond Siri: Here are the practical AI features coming to your iPhone in iOS 27
items.summary: Siri’s AI overhaul may have grabbed the headlines at WWDC, but some of Apple’s most useful AI features are arriving elsewhere in iOS 27.

items.title: Signal’s Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots ‘are not your friends’
items.summary: "These are not your friends. These are not conscious beings. These are not sentient interlocutors.”

items.title: In the Weights is your new AI-centric vanity search
items.summary: So ... what's your In the Weights score?

items.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
items.summary: Atlantic reporter Alex Reisner recently uncovered four datasets of music being used to train AI models and made them fully searchable for the public. Two of the sets are absolutely enormous at 12 million and 9 million tracks. The other two are much smaller, but still represent a significant amount o

items.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
items.summary: Jumper isn't the only big name leaving Google DeepMind.

items.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn’t stop anyone
items.summary: For the last 30 years, stopping the flow of cybersecurity-related software has proven to be ineffective. It's unclear why it would work now with Anthropic’s cybersecurity model Mythos.

items.title: IEEE Rolls Out Large Language Models Virtual Training Course
items.summary: Large language models have moved out of the research lab and into engineers’ daily workflow. LLMs serve as reasoning engines that can orchestrate complex tasks including identifying vulnerabilities in source code and transforming fragmented project discussions into rigorous technical specifications.

items.title: Is the US government’s Anthropic ban accidentally helping the brand?

items.title: Billionaire Ambani wants AI in every call, app, and home
items.summary: Reliance is weaving AI into telecom services used by more than 500 million people.

items.title: The film about Sam Altman has been dropped by Amazon MGM
items.summary: Luca Guadagnino's film about OpenAI CEO Sam Altman, Artificial, has reportedly been dropped by Amazon MGM. items.title: The CEO of Allbirds’ new AI biz has a plan, but no team
items.summary: Call it a startup with a sole founder and a very large seed round, but what's next is less clear.

items.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
items.summary: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. But Subquadratic has sta

items.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
items.summary: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. items.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
items.summary: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him. Harrell is paralyzed and unable to speak coherently without the device. He has now spent almost three years using a brain-computer interf

items.title: The US says ASML’s top chip tool may be in China, but how?
items.summary: There's a commercial logic that cuts against the idea that ASML would risk its export license to arm a Chinese customer.

items.title: Barret Zoph is out at OpenAI again after just five months
items.summary: Five months after returning to OpenAI, Barret Zoph - the company's head of enterprise AI sales - has departed, The Verge has learned. Zoph returned to OpenAI in mid-January after a stint as co-founder and CTO of Thinking Machines Lab, the competing AI company founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati

items.title: Source: Elastic agrees to buy CRV-backed Deductive AI for up to $85M
items.summary: Deductive AI, a startup that uses AI to catch and resolve bugs in software, was founded just three years ago.

items.title: As China looms, Taiwan makes more drones for defense and the US military
items.summary: Taiwan's drone spending plans for defense could also boost business overseas.

items.title: AI inference startup Baseten reportedly raising $1.5B months after its last mega-round
items.summary: Startup Baseten is reportedly close to finalizing a $1.5 billion round at a $13 billion as the “inference gold rush" marches on.

items.title: Snap spins off AI video team into new company, Dotmo, due to costs
items.summary: The Snapchat maker is spinning off yet another internal unit. Dotmo will be composed of current Snap staff who are leaving the social media company to focus on AI video development.

items.title: How FERC’s Large-Load Interconnection Actions Help Address Grid Stress, Improve Affordability

items.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
items.summary: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

items.title: Almost half of US singles feel negatively about AI in dating, Match says
items.summary: About 47% of singles look negatively at the use of AI in dating -- but many dating app users are open to AI helping with profile punch-ups and conversation starters.

items.title: Amazon hopes to challenge Nvidia more directly by selling its AI chips
items.summary: AWS is in talks to sell its chips to other data centers. CEO Andy Jassy has said this represents a $50 billion opportunity for the company.
